title: Bayonetta

notes:
Everything now works perfectly, and performance is finally locked to 60fps (the max for the game) with all settings turned up* to max at 2560x1440. With previous versions of Proton with the same hardware and software (and inability to use Gallium 9 because of Nvidia drivers), the game would often drop to 40fps or less.

*Recompiling Proton with the patch from the winedb page for Bayonetta is required for setting different graphics settings. Because of having to use the patch, this is a Gold rating. This is the patch: https://bugs.winehq.org/attachment.cgi?id=58637
title: Bayonetta

notes: Wasn't working at first, then I updated my kernel to the latests and moved the game over to the drive with the main file system on, where steam is installed and it worked immediately without any launch settings. However, as others have mentioned, changing graphics settings makes the game act weird and makes it hang infinitely on launch after restart. You need to delete the "460790" folder in the steam "compatdata" folder, which contains the wine prefix to run the game, so it might also make you lose progress if you delete it. As of now I'm unsure how to change the game's graphics settings.
title: Bayonetta

notes: Bayonetta came bundled with Proton 3.7 (Valve pick), so consider this a PSA that it also works fine with 4.2-2. I've had a write error message after turning on SSAO in graphics settings, but after closing that window, it just continued saving and settings had been applied. Not an obstacle, nothing to tune, so still platinum in my book.

notes: The game was tested with proton 4.2-3. The menus, sound and fights work well. The performance is a bit low. There is a slight drop in frames when playing (2 to 4 frames) but it is playable. No adjustments were made in proton. In the game all the graphic options were downloaded.
title: Bayonetta

notes: ***Fix the saving issue with this patch (https://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=42982), and here's a guide on how to install custom Proton patches using Proton-tkg by TkGlitch (https://www.reddit.com/r/SteamPlay/comments/bgym4t/how_to_apply_a_wine_patch_to_proton_prefix/epqudl4/).*** - The game runs and runs with great performance out of the box, however you won't be able to change settings. The game will whine about not being able to save data, though you'll still be able to play. But the next time you try to launch the game, it'll just load to a black window. You'll need to delete the Proton prefix in "/path/to/steam library folder/steamapps/compatdata/460790". It's a folder just called "pfx" if you're unfamiliar. Install the patch mentioned above to fix this. Framerate stays at 60fps consistently, and cutscenes are locked at 30fps. This isn't anything to do with Proton, I just wanted to mention that in case you see it and panic. While I have not played the game myself on Windows it runs perfectly for me in the small hour I played, once the patch was applied. It also runs fine no matter what drive it's installed on, unlike with Windows where it whines if it's not in the default Steam install folder. tl;dr - install patch, game works at max settings, solid 60fps.
title: Bayonetta

notes: no extra configuration needed
title: Bayonetta

notes: Use the patch mentioned previously in other reports (either with ProtonTKG or otherwise) and you can adjust settings. Regardless, framerate is a solid 60fps, excluding cutscenes which are locked at 30fps. If the patch was not needed, this would be a Platinum title.
title: Bayonetta
